title: A2: Exploratory Visual Analysis
description: Transit and housing affordability — HTML report with captioned visualizations.
story: My first real dive into exploratory data analysis. I learned to ask questions of transit and housing data, then turn those questions into clear charts. The hardest part was choosing which encodings actually helped readers see the story.

title: A3: Visualization Critique & Redesign
description: COVID-19 spiral chart critique and grid redesign (sketches, final viz, writeup).
story: I took apart a confusing COVID spiral chart and rebuilt it as a simple grid. It taught me that 'clever' visualizations often hide more than they reveal, and that clarity beats novelty.

title: A4: Persuasive / Deceptive Viz
description: NYPD complaint accountability analysis with persuasive and earnest visualizations.
story: Building two views of the same data — one persuasive, one earnest — showed me how framing and design choices shape what people believe. Accountability starts with seeing the same numbers from more than one angle.
